zh.lifeSummary: Maud d'Chester於1071年出生在英國英格蘭爾切斯特布拉可。她於1089年與Harold de Sudeley在英國格盧斯特斯爾素德勒結婚。他們至少是8個兒子的父母。她去世於1147年享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: d'Chester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Chester, the county seat of Cheshire, or from any of various smaller places called with this word (as for example Little Chester in Derbyshire, Chester le Street in County Durham, or Chesters in Northumberland), which is from Old English ceaster ‘Roman fort or walled city’ (from Latin castra ‘legionary camp’).

variants.name: Cheshire
meanings: English: habitational name for someone from the county of Cheshire in northwestern England, the name of which is early recorded as Cestrescire, from the name of the county seat, Chester , + Old English scīr ‘district, division’.

variants.name: Chesterman
meanings: English: possibly a topographic name for someone who lived by a Roman fort, Old English ceaster, or a habitational name for someone from any of the places mentioned at Chester .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: Maud d'Chester was born in 1071, in Blacon, Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Harold de Sudeley in 1089, in Sudeley, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons. She died in 1147, at the age of 76.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud d'Chester(은)는 1071년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 체스터 블라콘에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1089년 영국 그루스스터실르 수데리에서 Harold de Sudeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1147년 76세의 나이로 사망했다.
